headlines.description: Manchester United wrapped up third spot with Sunday's entertaining 3-2 victory against Nottingham Forest as Bruno Fernandes' memorable season reached a new height.
headlines.shortLinkText: United wrap up third in eventful win over Forest

headlines.description: Dango Ouattara's late equaliser snatched a 2-2 draw for Brentford against Crystal Palace to keep their European dream alive.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brentford draw vs. Palace to stay in Europe hunt

headlines.description: Late goals from Enzo Le Fée and Wilson Isidor kept alive Sunderland's chances of European football as they came from behind to beat Everton 3-1 and ruin Toffees captain Seamus Coleman's final appearance at Hill Dickinson Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sunderland boost European hopes with 3-1 win

events.name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Leeds United

headlines.description: Dominic Calvert-Lewin struck a stoppage-time winner as Leeds dealt Brighton's hopes of European football next season a blow with a 1-0 win at Elland Road.
headlines.shortLinkText: Calvert-Lewin hands Leeds late win over Brighton

headlines.description: Fulham's hopes of finishing in Europe were dealt a huge blow as they drew 1-1 at relegated Wolves on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Fulham's European hopes dented in draw at Wolves

events.name: West Ham United at Newcastle United

headlines.description: Will Osula scored twice as Newcastle edged West Ham to the brink of relegation from the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham on the brink after defeat at Newcastle
